item.title: Traders Return to Fed Funds Wager That Surged During Repo Stress
item.description: Traders notched up record volumes in one narrow corner of the US interest-rate futures market this week, betting on a spread that reflects potential swings in overnight lending markets.

item.title: Texas Stock Exchange Adviser Expects First IPOs in Early 2027
item.description: The Texas Stock Exchange plans to host its first initial public offerings in the opening quarter of 2027 as it seeks to grab a chunk of business from Nasdaq and the New York Stock Exchange, said Jeb Hensarling, a strategic adviser to the Dallas-based marketplace.

item.title: Leftist Candidate in Copper Giant Peru Wants New Mining Rules
item.description: A leftist presidential candidate on the verge of reaching Peru’s runoff election is pledging to overhaul mining rules in one of the world’s leading copper-exporting countries.

item.title: Chile’s Flagship Bill to Set Back Efforts to Balance Budget
item.description: Chile’s new government sent its flagship economic bill to congress Wednesday, with the budget office forecasting it would erode public finances throughout the administration’s four years in office.

item.title: Rivian CEO Sees New Electric SUV as Key Milestone for AI Tech
item.description: Rivian Automotive Inc. is betting its next-generation R2 SUV will do more than boost sales. The higher-volume vehicle is key to unlocking the EV maker’s autonomous driving future, Chief Executive Officer RJ Scaringe said, by scaling the real-world data needed for its artificial-intelligence technology.

item.title: Brazil Oil Driller Expanding in Venezuela as US Eases Sanctions
item.description: A Brazilian oil company is expanding in Venezuela in a bet that sanctions relief will stimulate the country’s battered industry, highlighting regional appetite for participating in the US-led revival.

item.title: Yardeni, Who Pegged S&P 500 Nadir, Says War Risks Far From Over
item.description: Ed Yardeni has watched market cycles long enough to know when panic is peaking.

item.title: Mark Cuban-Led Sports Fund Has $450 Million in Commitments
item.description: A private equity fund focused on professional sports and led by Mark Cuban has secured $450 million in investor commitments at the first close of its flagship fund, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: South Africa Central Bank to Act If War Inflation Shock Persists
item.description: The South African Reserve Bank will respond to inflationary pressures triggered by the US-Israeli war on Iran if they prove persistent, Governor Lesetja Kganyago said.

item.title: DOJ Expected to Ease Marijuana Restrictions as Soon as Wednesday
item.description: The US Justice Department is expected to reclassify marijuana into a less restrictive federal category as soon as Wednesday, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: National Healthcare REIT Sinks 3.7% After $462 Million US IPO
item.description: National Healthcare Properties Inc., slumped 3.7% after the real estate investment trust raised $462 million in an initial public offering priced below the marketed range.

item.title: Ivory Coast Eyes Quarterly Price Reviews Amid Global Cocoa Crash
item.description: Ivory Coast is considering quarterly reviews of its domestic cocoa price as it looks to adjust to the huge slump in the international market and keep its beans competitive.

item.title: Trump Media CEO’s Exit Caps a 90% Drop as MAGA Stocks Stumble
item.description: Truth Social has rarely been more important on Wall Street, where traders hang on White House posts that can send the market tumbling or soaring back up again. The company’s stock, though, is much less in vogue.

item.title: Convenience Store Chain Yesway Climbs 10% After $280 Million IPO
item.description: Yesway Inc. shares jumped 10% in the company’s trading debut, after the convenience store operator raised $280 million in an upsized initial public offering.

item.title: Why Anthropic’s Mythos Is Sparking Global Alarm
item.description: Anthropic PBC has said its new artificial intelligence tool, Mythos, is too powerful to release to the general public. The AI giant has described the model as so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it will only be released to a limited number of carefully chosen parties. If tools like Mythos fall into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could provide attackers with a powerful new weapon to steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure.

item.title: Europe Diversifies Jet Fuel Supply But Import Pressure Is Rising
item.description: Europe will need to find abundant new supplies of jet fuel if it’s to maintain its usual level of imports over the summer holiday season.

item.title: Death of US Agents in Mexico Raises Fresh Sovereignty Concerns
item.description: The Mexican government said it was unaware that two US officials took part in an operation to dismantle a drug lab in northern Mexico, a development that threatens to escalate tensions with Washington over security and sovereignty.

item.title: EssilorLuxottica Posts 11% Sales Jump, Extends AI Glasses Boost
item.description: EssilorLuxottica SA delivered its third straight quarter of double-digit growth, reporting an 11% jump in constant-currency sales driven by its AI-glasses partnership with Meta Platforms Inc.

item.title: Oil Servicer Weatherford Sees War Impacts Deepening This Quarter
item.description: Weatherford International Plc., one of the world’s largest oilfield-service providers, is warning that a hit to earnings in the wake of the Iran war will get worse this quarter, before an eventual rebound.

item.title: Trump’s Shopping Spree for Equity Stakes Nears $21 Billion
item.description: The Trump administration’s $20.9 billion buying spree for equity stakes in private-sector companies is the biggest such push into strategic industries since the Second World War, according to research by the Council on Foreign Relations.

item.title: Bessent Says US Allies in Gulf, Asia Requested Swap Lines
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Wednesday that many Persian Gulf allies along with a number of Asian nations have requested foreign exchange swap lines with the US, and pointed to the potential for such arrangements to support dollar-denominated lending overseas.

item.title: Nvidia-Backed Vast Data Raises $1 Billion, Triples Valuation to $30 Billion
item.description: Fundraising round includes a secondary offering

item.title: Ping Pong Robot Uses Agentic AI to Beat Expert Human Players
item.description: Scientists at Sony AI have developed a table tennis robot with enough speed and precision to beat even some expert ping pong players in the latest matchup between biological and artificial intelligence.

item.title: BRB Shareholders Greenlight Capital Raise of Up to $1.8 Billion
item.description: Banco de Brasilia SA’s shareholders approved a capital raise of as much as 8.8 billion reais ($1.8 billion) to help bolster the Brazilian bank after it was stung by transactions it made with the failed Banco Master SA.

item.title: Pirro Says She Is Continuing to Probe Fed for Cost Overruns
item.description: US Attorney Jeanine Pirro said she is continuing her controversial investigation into building-renovation cost overruns by the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic at Near-Halt as Ships Come Under Gunfire
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz stayed at a near standstill Wednesday as Iranian forces opened fire on — and seized — commercial ships.

item.title: Petrobras Board Member Seeks Fuel Price Increase to Stem Losses
item.description: A Petrobras board member appointed by non-controlling shareholders is warning that the state-controlled producer is losing billions from below-market fuel sales and risks reputational damage.

item.title: Chipmakers on Track for Longest-Ever Rally Amid AI Optimism
item.description: An index tracking semiconductor stocks is on track for its all-time longest streak of daily gains, as investors anticipate strong growth for the sector driven by demand related to artificial intelligence.

item.title: Blackstone Private Credit Fund Looks to Sell High-Grade Bonds
item.description: Blackstone Private Credit Fund is selling investment-grade notes, as private lenders known as business development companies ramp up debt issuance after a dry spell.

item.title: How Microsoft Spooked the Global Carbon Removal Market
item.description: In the early days of April, a group of mid-level employees at Microsoft Corp. started making calls that would send tremors through the global market for carbon removals.
